summary.intent: Address a second round of Codex review findings on PR #694 in the ars-ui repo, fixing accessibility and behavioral issues in RatingGroup and TagGroup components, then get Codex approval.
summary.outcome: All five new Codex findings were patched (RatingGroup hover/focus preservation, aria-readonly, label attribute revert; TagGroup controlled selection internal state), full CI coverage passed at 98.5% lines / 90.1% branch, commit d077a27e (and a follow-up) were pushed, threads were resolved, and Codex was re-triggered.

repo: The repo uses `cargo xtask ci coverage` for a full multi-crate coverage gate with a 90% branch threshold; the merged threshold table is the final signal.
Snapshots are managed with `cargo insta accept` and must be re-accepted after intentional behavioral reverts before tests go green.
Codex review is triggered via `gh pr comment 694 --body '@codex review'` and signals active review with an 'eyes' reaction; findings arrive as new unresolved review threads.
The `snapshot-reviewed` label must be re-applied after each push that changes snapshots.
The coverage suite has distinct native (nextest), wasm DOM, i18n, Leptos wasm, Dioxus wasm, and adapter harness phases that run sequentially.
The `cargo xtask spec validate` and `cargo xtask spec compile-snippets` commands are used to validate spec markdown contracts independently of unit tests.

code.finding: RatingGroup control attrs wire aria-labelledby to the visible label part; the core cannot know if the optional adapter label is rendered, so the always-on label reference was reverted.
Hover exit on RatingGroup must preserve focus state rather than resetting it.
aria-readonly should be emitted on the RatingGroup control element.
Controlled TagGroup selection must keep pending internal value without mutating the exposed controlled value; sync_selected_keys is the relevant function to patch.
The spec contract for TagGroup controlled selection and requested_selected_keys/removed_keys/Effect behavior needed updating to match the new internal-state approach.

workflow: Codex review threads are resolved via GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation using thread IDs extracted from the PR's reviewThreads query.
Polling Codex review status requires checking both the reactions endpoint (eyes = in progress) and the unresolved threads GraphQL query together.
After each fix push, reply to each original Codex comment thread with the fix commit SHA before resolving, to provide traceability.
Running `cargo +nightly fmt --all` before committing is part of the expected CI-passing workflow.

friction: Multiple long sleep-poll loops (60s, 120s×3) were needed to wait for Codex review completion with no event-driven mechanism available.
`cargo insta accept` had to be run twice before tests went green, suggesting snapshot state was not fully settled after the first accept.
The spec validate command required a separate `compile-snippets` pass after touching spec markdown, adding an extra step to the fix cycle.
Determining the correct RatingGroup label-attribute behavior required reading both the spec and the connect.rs/component source before concluding the always-on reference had to be reverted.
open_items: Codex re-review is still in progress at the end of the session; approval or further findings are pending.
Unit Tests CI check was still in progress when the session ended; final green status not confirmed.
The optional-label pattern for RatingGroup (core cannot know if adapter label is rendered) may need a more principled long-term solution rather than a revert.
